c74object.name: cropsilence~
c74object.digest: Crop silent segments from buffers in a polybuffer~
c74object.description: The `cropsilence~` object scans each buffer within a `polybuffer~` in segments equal to a specified bar length. If a segment's peak amplitude is below a noise floor of 0.00009, it is cropped. The remaining segments are concatenated, and the buffer is resized to a multiple of the bar length. The cropping process is asynchronous and performed in a background thread to avoid blocking the main thread during heavy processing.

inlet.digest: Inlet 1 (float/int/symbol): Bar length (ms) or Bind message
inlet.description: A number (either integer or floating-point) in this inlet triggers the cropping process for all buffers in the polybuffer~ using the specified bar length in milliseconds. A 'bind' message followed by a symbol binds the object to a polybuffer~ and stores its initial state as a ground truth.

outlet.description: Outputs a bang when the asynchronous cropping process for all buffers is complete.
When the @log attribute is enabled, this outlet provides detailed diagnostic information about buffer binding and the cropping process.

method.name: float
method.description: Starts the cropping process using the provided bar length in milliseconds. The cropping is always performed relative to the original ground truth state of the buffers stored during the last 'bind' message.

method.name: int
method.description: Starts the cropping process using the provided bar length in milliseconds. The cropping is always performed relative to the original ground truth state of the buffers stored during the last 'bind' message.

method.name: bind
method.description: Binds the object to the specified `polybuffer~`. Upon the first successful bind to a specific name, the object creates an internal copy of all buffers in that `polybuffer~` to serve as the ground truth for all future cropping operations. Subsequent 'bind' messages with the same name will not overwrite the ground truth.

attribute.name: log
attribute.description: When enabled (1), detailed status information is sent to the second outlet.
